evals.prompt: I have a mixed Python/Shell project. Codacy is flagging ruff findings in .py files and shellcheck findings in .sh files on the same PR. How do I triage both languages in a single pass without switching contexts?
evals.expected_output: Use the Linter Tool Registry to identify per-language tools, run targeted linters on changed files only, classify findings by severity, and batch fixes by language.
assertions: references the Linter Tool Registry or lists per-language tools (ruff for Python, shellcheck for Shell)
recommends running linters on changed files only (not full repo)
classifies findings by severity (Error blocks merge, Warning is advisory)

evals.prompt: I need to suppress a shellcheck finding where word splitting is intentional. The skill mandates a specific format for suppressions — what is it and why does it matter?
evals.expected_output: Use the [tool-disable] format with rule-id, reason, agent-id, and date. This format is required for auditability and traceability.
assertions: uses the [tool-disable] format (not just # shellcheck disable)
includes all required fields: rule-id, reason, agent-id, date
explains why this format matters (auditability, traceability)

evals.prompt: After fixing lint errors locally, what should I do before pushing? Walk me through the pre-commit workflow.
evals.expected_output: Run quality_gate.sh, verify zero warnings, scan staged changes for secrets, then commit. The skill has a specific pre-commit workflow with these steps.
assertions: references quality_gate.sh as the verification step
mentions scanning staged changes for secrets
follows the Pre-commit Workflow section's step sequence
